title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Recovery Case: Collaboration Communication
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git collaboration communication increment in a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Collaboration Communication
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Collaboration Communication contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.

content.text: A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Collaboration Communication condition decided your prediction.

options.text: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
A force-push warning in chat is sufficient coordination regardless of response. This does not resolve predict collaboration communication.

For predict collaboration communication, apply Collaboration Communication without its stated preconditions, omit the a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Collaboration Communication for a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.text: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Collaboration Communication while producing a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v4.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. Competency probe: Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people. A defensible solution applies Collaboration Communication, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-collaboration-communication — Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
# Evidence: changed Git case 5
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A force-push warning in chat is sufficient coordination regardless of response. This fails the Collaboration Communication contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
